v.KJV: And it came to pass afterward, that he went throughout every city and village, preaching and shewing the glad tidings of the kingdom of God: and the twelve were with him,

v.NIV: After this, Jesus traveled about from one town and village to another, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom of God. The Twelve were with him,

v.CNV: 还有几个蒙了医治、脱离污鬼与疾病的妇女，其中有称为抹大拉的马利亚，曾有七个鬼从她身上赶出来；

v.KJV: And certain women, which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities, Mary called Magdalene, out of whom went seven devils,
v.NASB: and also some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses: Mary who was called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,
v.NIV: and also some women who had been cured of evil spirits and diseases: Mary (called Magdalene) from whom seven demons had come out;
v.NRSV: as well as some women who had been cured of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary, called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,

v.WLB: 악한 영과 질병으로부터 고침받은 여자들도 예수와 함께했습니다. 이들은 일곱 귀신이 떠나간 막달라 마리아였고

v.CNV: 有希律的管家古撒的妻子约亚拿，又有苏珊娜，和许多别的妇女，她们都用自己的财物供给耶稣和门徒。
v.ESV: and Joanna, the wife of Chuza, Herod's household manager, and Susanna, and many others, who provided for them out of their means.

v.KJV: And Joanna the wife of Chuza Herod’s steward, and Susanna, and many others, which ministered unto him of their substance.
v.NASB: and Joanna the wife of Chuza, Herod's steward, and Susanna, and many others who were contributing to their support out of their private means.
v.NIV: Joanna the wife of Chuza, the manager of Herod’s household; Susanna; and many others. These women were helping to support them out of their own means.
v.NRSV: and Joanna, the wife of Herod’s steward Chuza, and Susanna, and many others, who provided for them out of their resources.

v.CNV: 当时有许多人聚在一起，还有人从各城来到耶稣那里，他就用比喻说：
v.ESV: And when a great crowd was gathering and people from town after town came to him, he said in a parable,

v.KJV: And when much people were gathered together, and were come to him out of every city, he spake by a parable:
v.NASB: When a large crowd was coming together, and those from the various cities were journeying to Him, He spoke by way of a parable:
v.NIV: While a large crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from town after town, he told this parable:
v.NRSV: When a great crowd gathered and people from town after town came to him, he said in a parable:

v.WLB: 많은 무리가 모여들고 각 마을에서 사람들이 예수께로 나아오니 예수께서 그들에게 비유를 통해 말씀하셨습니다.

v.CNV: “有一个撒种的出去撒种，撒的时候，有的落在路旁，被人践踏，或给空中的小鸟吃掉。
v.ESV: “A sower went out to sow his seed. And as he sowed, some fell along the path and was trampled underfoot, and the birds of the air devoured it.
v.GAE: 씨를 뿌리는 자가 그 씨를 뿌리러 나가서 뿌릴새 더러는 길 가에 떨어지매 밟히며 공중의 새들이 먹어버렸고
v.KJV: A sower went out to sow his seed: and as he sowed, some fell by the way side; and it was trodden down, and the fowls of the air devoured it.
v.NASB: 'The sower went out to sow his seed; and as he sowed, some fell beside the road, and it was trampled under foot and the birds of the air ate it up.
v.NIV: “A farmer went out to sow his seed. As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path; it was trampled on, and the birds ate it up.
v.NRSV: “A sower went out to sow his seed; and as he sowed, some fell on the path and was trampled on, and the birds of the air ate it up.
v.SAENEW: "씨 뿌리는 사람이 씨를 뿌리러 나갔다. 그가 씨를 뿌리는데, 더러는 길가에 떨어지니, 발에 밟히기도 하고, 하늘의 새들이 쪼아먹기도 하였다.
v.WLB: “씨 뿌리는 사람이 씨를 뿌리러 나갔다. 그가 씨를 뿌리자 그중 어떤 씨는 길가에 떨어져 사람들에게 밟히고 하늘의 새들에게 다 먹혀 버렸다.

v.CNV: 有的落在石头地上，一长出来就枯萎了，因为得不着滋润。
v.ESV: And some fell on the rock, and as it grew up, it withered away, because it had no moisture.
v.GAE: 더러는 바위 위에 떨어지매 싹이 났다가 습기가 없으므로 말랐고
v.KJV: And some fell upon a rock; and as soon as it was sprung up, it withered away, because it lacked moisture.
v.NASB: 'Other seed fell on rocky soil, and as soon as it grew up, it withered away, because it had no moisture.
v.NIV: Some fell on rocky ground, and when it came up, the plants withered because they had no moisture.
v.NRSV: Some fell on the rock; and as it grew up, it withered for lack of moisture.
v.SAENEW: 또 더러는 돌짝밭에 떨어지니, 싹이 돋아났다가 물기가 없어서 말라 버렸다.
v.WLB: 어떤 씨는 바위 위에 떨어졌는데 싹이 돋았지만 물기가 없어서 곧 시들어 버리고 말았다.

v.CNV: 有的落在荆棘丛中，荆棘也一齐生长，把它挤住了。
v.ESV: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up with it and choked it.
v.GAE: 더러는 가시떨기 속에 떨어지매 가시가 함께 자라서 기운을 막았고
v.KJV: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprang up with it, and choked it.
v.NASB: 'Other seed fell among the thorns; and the thorns grew up with it and choked it out.
v.NIV: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up with it and choked the plants.
v.NRSV: Some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew with it and choked it.
v.SAENEW: 또 더러는 가시덤불 속에 떨어지니, 가시덤불이 함께 자라서, 그 기운을 막았다.
v.WLB: 어떤 씨는 가시덤불 속에 떨어졌는데 가시덤불이 함께 자라서 그 기운을 막아 버렸다.

v.CNV: 有的落在好土里，就生长起来，结出百倍的果实。”他说了这些话，就大声说：“有耳可听的，就应当听！”
v.ESV: And some fell into good soil and grew and yielded a hundredfold.” As he said these things, he called out, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”
v.GAE: 더러는 좋은 땅에 떨어지매 나서 백 배의 결실을 하였느니라 이 말씀을 하시고 외치시되 들을 귀 있는 자는 들을지어다
v.KJV: And other fell on good ground, and sprang up, and bare fruit an hundredfold. And when he had said these things, he cried, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
v.NASB: 'Other seed fell into the good soil, and grew up, and produced a crop a hundred times as great.' As He said these things, He would call out, 'He who has ears to hear, let him hear.'
v.NIV: Still other seed fell on good soil. It came up and yielded a crop, a hundred times more than was sown.” When he said this, he called out, “Whoever has ears to hear, let them hear.”
v.NRSV: Some fell into good soil, and when it grew, it produced a hundredfold.” As he said this, he called out, “Let anyone with ears to hear listen!”
v.SAENEW: 그런데 더러는 좋은 땅에 떨어져서 자라나, 백 배의 열매를 맺었다." 이 말씀을 하시고, 예수께서는 "들을 귀가 있는 사람은 들어라" 하고 외치셨다.
v.WLB: 또 다른 씨는 좋은 땅에 떨어져 자라나 100배나 많은 열매를 맺었다.” 예수께서 말씀을 마치고 큰 소리로 외치셨습니다. “들을 귀 있는 사람은 들으라!”

v.CNV: 门徒问他这比喻是什么意思。
v.ESV: And when his disciples asked him what this parable meant,

v.NASB: His disciples began questioning Him as to what this parable meant.
v.NIV: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
v.NRSV: Then his disciples asked him what this parable meant.

v.CNV: 他说：“　神的国的奥秘，只给你们知道，对别人就用比喻，叫他们看却看不见，听却听不明白。
v.ESV: he said, “To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of God, but for others they are in parables, so that ‘seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.’
v.GAE: 이르시되 하나님 나라의 비밀을 아는 것이 너희에게는 허락되었으나 다른 사람에게는 비유로 하나니 이는 그들로 보아도 보지 못하고 들어도 깨닫지 못하게 하려 함이라
v.KJV: And he said, Unto you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God: but to others in parables; that seeing they might not see, and hearing they might not understand.
v.NASB: And He said, 'To you it has been granted to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God, but to the rest it is in parables, so that SEEING THEY MAY NOT SEE, AND HEARING THEY MAY NOT UNDERSTAND.
v.NIV: He said, “The knowledge of the secrets of the kingdom of God has been given to you, but to others I speak in parables, so that, “‘though seeing, they may not see; though hearing, they may not understand.’
v.NRSV: He said, “To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of God; but to others I speak in parables, so that ‘looking they may not perceive, and listening they may not understand.’
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 대답하셨다. "너희에게는 하나님 나라의 비밀을 아는 것을 허락해 주셨다. 그러나 다른 사람들에게는 비유로 말하였으니, 그것은 '그들이 보아도 보지 못하고, 들어도 깨닫지 못하게 하려는 것'이다."
v.WLB: 예수께서 말씀하셨습니다. “너희에게는 하나님 나라의 비밀을 알게 해 주었다. 그러나 내가 다른 사람들에게는 비유로 말했다. 이것은 ‘그들이 보아도 보지 못하고 들어도 깨닫지 못하게’하려는 것이다.

v.CNV: “这比喻是说，种子是　神的道，
v.ESV: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
v.GAE: 이 비유는 이러하니라 씨는 하나님의 말씀이요
v.KJV: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
v.NASB: 'Now the parable is this: the seed is the word of God.
v.NIV: “This is the meaning of the parable: The seed is the word of God.
v.NRSV: “Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
v.SAENEW: "그 비유의 뜻은 이러하다. 씨는 하나님의 말씀이다.
v.WLB: 비유의 뜻은 이와 같다. 씨는 하나님의 말씀이다.

v.CNV: 那落在路旁的，就是人听了，魔鬼随即来到，从他们心里把道夺去，恐怕他们相信就得救了。
v.ESV: The ones along the path are those who have heard; then the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved.
v.GAE: 길 가에 있다는 것은 말씀을 들은 자니 이에 마귀가 가서 그들이 믿어 구원을 얻지 못하게 하려고 말씀을 그 마음에서 빼앗는 것이요
v.KJV: Those by the way side are they that hear; then cometh the devil, and taketh away the word out of their hearts, lest they should believe and be saved.
v.NASB: 'Those beside the road are those who have heard; then the devil comes and takes away the word from their heart, so that they will not believe and be saved.
v.NIV: Those along the path are the ones who hear, and then the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved.
v.NRSV: The ones on the path are those who have heard; then the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved.
v.SAENEW: 길가에 떨어진 것들은, 말씀을 듣기는 하였으나, 그 뒤에 악마가 와서, 그들의 마음에서 말씀을 빼앗아 가므로, 믿지 못하고 구원을 받지 못하게 되는 사람들이다.
v.WLB: 길가에 떨어진 것은 하나님의 말씀을 들었으나 마귀가 와서 그 마음에서 말씀을 빼앗아 가는 바람에 믿지 못하고 구원받지 못하는 사람들이다.

v.CNV: 那落在石头地上的，就是人听了，欢欢喜喜地接受，但是没有根，不过是暂时相信，一旦遭遇试炼，就倒退了。
v.ESV: And the ones on the rock are those who, when they hear the word, receive it with joy. But these have no root; they believe for a while, and in time of testing fall away.
v.GAE: 바위 위에 있다는 것은 말씀을 들을 때에 기쁨으로 받으나 뿌리가 없어 잠깐 믿다가 시련을 당할 때에 배반하는 자요
v.KJV: They on the rock are they, which, when they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no root, which for a while believe, and in time of temptation fall away.
v.NASB: 'Those on the rocky soil are those who, when they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no firm root; they believe for a while, and in time of temptation fall away.
v.NIV: Those on the rocky ground are the ones who receive the word with joy when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while, but in the time of testing they fall away.
v.NRSV: The ones on the rock are those who, when they hear the word, receive it with joy. But these have no root; they believe only for a while and in a time of testing fall away.
v.SAENEW: 돌짝밭에 떨어진 것들은, 들을 때에는 그 말씀을 기쁘게 받아들이지만, 뿌리가 없으므로 잠시 동안 믿다가, 시련의 때가 오면 떨어져 나가는 사람들이다.
v.WLB: 바위 위에 떨어진 것은 하나님의 말씀을 듣고 기쁘게 받아들이지만 뿌리가 없어 잠시 동안 믿다가 시련이 닥치면 곧 넘어지는 사람들이다.

v.CNV: 那落在荆棘里的，就是人听了，走开以后，被今世的忧虑、财富和宴乐挤住了，结不出成熟的子粒来。
v.ESV: And as for what fell among the thorns, they are those who hear, but as they go on their way they are choked by the cares and riches and pleasures of life, and their fruit does not mature.
v.GAE: 가시떨기에 떨어졌다는 것은 말씀을 들은 자이나 지내는 중 이생의 염려와 재물과 향락에 기운이 막혀 온전히 결실하지 못하는 자요
v.KJV: And that which fell among thorns are they, which, when they have heard, go forth, and are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruit to perfection.
v.NASB: 'The seed which fell among the thorns, these are the ones who have heard, and as they go on their way they are choked with worries and riches and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruit to maturity.
v.NIV: The seed that fell among thorns stands for those who hear, but as they go on their way they are choked by life’s worries, riches and pleasures, and they do not mature.
v.NRSV: As for what fell among the thorns, these are the ones who hear; but as they go on their way, they are choked by the cares and riches and pleasures of life, and their fruit does not mature.
v.SAENEW: 가시덤불에 떨어진 것들은, 말씀을 들었으나, 살아가는 동안에 근심과 재물과 인생의 향락에 사로잡혀서, 열매를 맺는 데에 이르지 못하는 사람들이다.
v.WLB: 가시밭에 떨어진 것은 하나님의 말씀을 들었으나 이 세상의 걱정과 부와 쾌락에 사로잡혀서 자라지 못하고 온전한 열매를 맺지 못하는 사람들이다.

v.CNV: 但那落在好土里的，就是人用诚实良善的心来听，把道持守住，忍耐着结出果实。
v.ESV: As for that in the good soil, they are those who, hearing the word, hold it fast in an honest and good heart, and bear fruit with patience.
v.GAE: 좋은 땅에 있다는 것은 착하고 좋은 마음으로 말씀을 듣고 지키어 인내로 결실하는 자니라
v.KJV: But that on the good ground are they, which in an honest and good heart, having heard the word, keep it, and bring forth fruit with patience.
v.NASB: 'But the seed in the good soil, these are the ones who have heard the word in an honest and good heart, and hold it fast, and bear fruit with perseverance.
v.NIV: But the seed on good soil stands for those with a noble and good heart, who hear the word, retain it, and by persevering produce a crop.
v.NRSV: But as for that in the good soil, these are the ones who, when they hear the word, hold it fast in an honest and good heart, and bear fruit with patient endurance.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 좋은 땅에 떨어진 것들은, 바르고 착한 마음으로 말씀을 듣고서, 그것을 굳게 간직하여 견디는 가운데 열매를 맺는 사람들이다."
v.WLB: 그러나 좋은 땅에 떨어진 것은 착하고 좋은 마음으로 하나님의 말씀을 들은 뒤 그 말씀을 굳게 간직하고 인내해 좋은 열매를 맺는 사람들이다.

v.CNV: “没有人点灯用器皿盖上，或放在床底下，而是放在灯台上，叫进来的人都看得见光。
v.ESV: “No one after lighting a lamp covers it with a jar or puts it under a bed, but puts it on a stand, so that those who enter may see the light.
v.GAE: 누구든지 등불을 켜서 그릇으로 덮거나 평상 아래에 두지 아니하고 등경 위에 두나니 이는 들어가는 자들로 그 빛을 보게 하려 함이라
v.KJV: No man, when he hath lighted a candle, covereth it with a vessel, or putteth it under a bed; but setteth it on a candlestick, that they which enter in may see the light.
v.NASB: 'Now no one after lighting a lamp covers it over with a container, or puts it under a bed; but he puts it on a lampstand, so that those who come in may see the light.
v.NIV: “No one lights a lamp and hides it in a clay jar or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on a stand, so that those who come in can see the light.
v.NRSV: “No one after lighting a lamp hides it under a jar, or puts it under a bed, but puts it on a lampstand, so that those who enter may see the light.
v.SAENEW: "아무도 등불을 켜서, 그릇으로 덮거나, 침대 아래에다 놓지 않고, 등경 위에다가 올려놓아서, 들어오는 사람들이 그 빛을 보게 한다.
v.WLB: 등불을 켜서 그릇으로 덮거나 침대 밑에 두는 사람은 아무도 없다. 오히려 등불은 들어오는 사람들이 볼 수 있도록 등잔대 위에 두는 것이다.

v.CNV: 因为没有什么隐藏的事不被显明，也没有什么掩盖的事不被人知道而暴露出来。
v.ESV: For nothing is hidden that will not be made manifest, nor is anything secret that will not be known and come to light.
v.GAE: 숨은 것이 장차 드러나지 아니할 것이 없고 감추인 것이 장차 알려지고 나타나지 않을 것이 없느니라
v.KJV: For nothing is secret, that shall not be made manifest; neither any thing hid, that shall not be known and come abroad.
v.NASB: 'For nothing is hidden that will not become evident, nor anything secret that will not be known and come to light.
v.NIV: For there is nothing hidden that will not be disclosed, and nothing concealed that will not be known or brought out into the open.
v.NRSV: For nothing is hidden that will not be disclosed, nor is anything secret that will not become known and come to light.
v.SAENEW: 숨겨 둔 것은 드러나고, 감추어 둔 것은 알려져서 환히 나타나기 마련이다.
v.WLB: 숨겨 둔 것은 드러나고 감춰 둔 것은 알려지게 마련이다.

v.CNV: 所以你们应当留心怎样听，因为凡是有的，还要给他；凡是没有的，连他自以为有的，也要拿去。”
v.ESV: Take care then how you hear, for to the one who has, more will be given, and from the one who has not, even what he thinks that he has will be taken away.”
v.GAE: 그러므로 너희가 어떻게 들을까 스스로 삼가라 누구든지 있는 자는 받겠고 없는 자는 그 있는 줄로 아는 것까지도 빼앗기리라 하시니라
v.KJV: Take heed therefore how ye hear: for whosoever hath, to him shall be given; and whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he seemeth to have.
v.NASB: 'So take care how you listen; for whoever has, to him more shall be given; and whoever does not have, even what he thinks he has shall be taken away from him.'
v.NIV: Therefore consider carefully how you listen. Whoever has will be given more; whoever does not have, even what they think they have will be taken from them.”
v.NRSV: Then pay attention to how you listen; for to those who have, more will be given; and from those who do not have, even what they seem to have will be taken away.”
v.SAENEW: 그러므로 너희는 조심하여 들어라. 가진 사람은 더 받을 것이요, 가지지 못한 사람은 가진 줄로 생각하는 것마저 빼앗길 것이다."
v.WLB: 그러므로 너희는 내 말을 조심해서 들으라. 가진 사람은 더 받고 가지지 못한 사람은 가진 줄로 생각하는 것조차 빼앗길 것이다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣的母亲和弟弟来到他那里，因为人多，不能到他跟前。
v.ESV: Then his mother and his brothers came to him, but they could not reach him because of the crowd.
v.GAE: 예수의 어머니와 그 동생들이 왔으나 무리로 인하여 가까이 하지 못하니
v.KJV: Then came to him his mother and his brethren, and could not come at him for the press.
v.NASB: And His mother and brothers came to Him, and they were unable to get to Him because of the crowd.
v.NIV: Now Jesus’ mother and brothers came to see him, but they were not able to get near him because of the crowd.
v.NRSV: Then his mother and his brothers came to him, but they could not reach him because of the crowd.
v.SAENEW: 예수의 어머니와 형제들이 예수께로 왔으나, 무리 때문에 만날 수 없었다.
v.WLB: 예수의 어머니와 형제들이 예수께로 왔으나 많은 사람들 때문에 예수께 가까이 갈 수가 없었습니다.

v.CNV: 有人转告耶稣：“你母亲和弟弟站在外面要见你。”
v.ESV: And he was told, “Your mother and your brothers are standing outside, desiring to see you.”
v.GAE: 어떤 이가 알리되 당신의 어머니와 동생들이 당신을 보려고 밖에 서 있나이다
v.KJV: And it was told him by certain which said, Thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to see thee.
v.NASB: And it was reported to Him, 'Your mother and Your brothers are standing outside, wishing to see You.'
v.NIV: Someone told him, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.”
v.NRSV: And he was told, “Your mother and your brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.”
v.SAENEW: 그래서 사람들이 예수께 전하였다. "선생님의 어머니와 형제들이 밖에 서서, 선생님을 만나고 싶어합니다."
v.WLB: 그래서 사람들이 예수께 말했습니다. “선생님의 어머니와 형제들이 선생님을 만나려고 밖에 서 있습니다.”

v.CNV: 他回答他们：“听了　神的道而遵行的人，才是我的母亲，我的弟兄。”
v.ESV: But he answered them, “My mother and my brothers are those who hear the word of God and do it.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 대답하여 이르시되 내 어머니와 내 동생들은 곧 하나님의 말씀을 듣고 행하는 이 사람들이라 하시니라
v.KJV: And he answered and said unto them, My mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of God, and do it.
v.NASB: But He answered and said to them, 'My mother and My brothers are these who hear the word of God and do it.'
v.NIV: He replied, “My mother and brothers are those who hear God’s word and put it into practice.”
v.NRSV: But he said to them, “My mother and my brothers are those who hear the word of God and do it.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "하나님의 말씀을 듣고 행하는 이 사람들이 나의 어머니요, 나의 형제들이다."
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “하나님의 말씀을 듣고 실천하는 사람이 바로 내 어머니요, 내 형제들이다.”

v.CNV: 有一天，耶稣和门徒上了船，他对他们说：“我们渡到海那边去吧。”他们就开了船。
v.ESV: One day he got into a boat with his disciples, and he said to them, “Let us go across to the other side of the lake.” So they set out,
v.GAE: 하루는 제자들과 함께 배에 오르사 그들에게 이르시되 호수 저편으로 건너가자 하시매 이에 떠나
v.KJV: Now it came to pass on a certain day, that he went into a ship with his disciples: and he said unto them, Let us go over unto the other side of the lake. And they launched forth.
v.NASB: Now on one of those days Jesus and His disciples got into a boat, and He said to them, 'Let us go over to the other side of the lake.' So they launched out.
v.NIV: One day Jesus said to his disciples, “Let us go over to the other side of the lake.” So they got into a boat and set out.
v.NRSV: One day he got into a boat with his disciples, and he said to them, “Let us go across to the other side of the lake.” So they put out,
v.SAENEW: 어느 날 예수께서 제자들과 함께 배에 오르셔서, 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "호수 저쪽으로 건너가자." 그들이 출발하여
v.WLB: 하루는 예수께서 제자들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “호수 저편으로 가자.” 그래서 그들은 배에 올라타고 떠났습니다.

v.CNV: 船行的时候，他睡着了。海上忽然起了狂风，他们全身湿透，非常危险。
v.ESV: and as they sailed he fell asleep. And a windstorm came down on the lake, and they were filling with water and were in danger.
v.GAE: 행선할 때에 예수께서 잠이 드셨더니 마침 광풍이 호수로 내리치매 배에 물이 가득하게 되어 위태한지라
v.KJV: But as they sailed he fell asleep: and there came down a storm of wind on the lake; and they were filled with water, and were in jeopardy.
v.NASB: But as they were sailing along He fell asleep; and a fierce gale of wind descended on the lake, and they began to be swamped and to be in danger.
v.NIV: As they sailed, he fell asleep. A squall came down on the lake, so that the boat was being swamped, and they were in great danger.
v.NRSV: and while they were sailing he fell asleep. A windstorm swept down on the lake, and the boat was filling with water, and they were in danger.
v.SAENEW: 배를 저어 가고 있을 때에 예수께서는 잠이 드셨다. 그런데 사나운 바람이 호수로 내리 불어서, 배에 물이 차고, 그들은 위태롭게 되었다.
v.WLB: 그들이 배를 저어 가는 동안 예수께서는 잠이 드셨습니다. 그때 호수에 사나운 바람이 불어오더니 배에 물이 들이쳐 매우 위험한 상황이 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 门徒来叫醒耶稣，说：“主啊！主啊！我们没命了！”他醒过来，斥责风浪，风浪就止息、平静了。
v.ESV: And they went and woke him, saying, “Master, Master, we are perishing!” And he awoke and rebuked the wind and the raging waves, and they ceased, and there was a calm.
v.GAE: 제자들이 나아와 깨워 이르되 주여 주여 우리가 죽겠나이다 한대 예수께서 잠을 깨사 바람과 물결을 꾸짖으시니 이에 그쳐 잔잔하여지더라
v.KJV: And they came to him, and awoke him, saying, Master, master, we perish. Then he arose, and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water: and they ceased, and there was a calm.
v.NASB: They came to Jesus and woke Him up, saying, 'Master, Master, we are perishing!' And He got up and rebuked the wind and the surging waves, and they stopped, and it became calm.
v.NIV: The disciples went and woke him, saying, “Master, Master, we’re going to drown!” He got up and rebuked the wind and the raging waters; the storm subsided, and all was calm.
v.NRSV: They went to him and woke him up, shouting, “Master, Master, we are perishing!” And he woke up and rebuked the wind and the raging waves; they ceased, and there was a calm.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 제자들이 다가가서 예수를 깨우고서 말하였다. "선생님, 선생님, 우리가 죽게 되었습니다." 예수께서 깨어나서, 바람과 성난 물결을 꾸짖으시니, 바람과 물결이 곧 그치고 잔잔해졌다.
v.WLB: 제자들이 가서 예수를 깨우며 말했습니다. “선생님! 선생님! 우리가 모두 빠져 죽게 생겼습니다!” 그러자 예수께서 일어나 바람과 파도를 꾸짖으시니 폭풍이 멈추고 호수가 다시 잔잔해졌습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣对他们说：“你们的信心在哪里？”他们又惧怕、又希奇，彼此说：“这到底是谁？他吩咐风浪，连风浪也听从他。”
v.ESV: He said to them, “Where is your faith?” And they were afraid, and they marveled, saying to one another, “Who then is this, that he commands even winds and water, and they obey him?”
v.GAE: 제자들에게 이르시되 너희 믿음이 어디 있느냐 하시니 그들이 두려워하고 놀랍게 여겨 서로 말하되 그가 누구이기에 바람과 물을 명하매 순종하는가 하더라
v.KJV: And he said unto them, Where is your faith? And they being afraid wondered, saying one to another, What manner of man is this! for he commandeth even the winds and water, and they obey him.
v.NASB: And He said to them, 'Where is your faith?' They were fearful and amazed, saying to one another, 'Who then is this, that He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey Him?'
v.NIV: “Where is your faith?” he asked his disciples. In fear and amazement they asked one another, “Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.”
v.NRSV: He said to them, “Where is your faith?” They were afraid and amazed, and said to one another, “Who then is this, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "너희의 믿음이 어디에 있느냐?" 그들은 두려워하였고, 놀라서 서로 말하였다. "이분이 도대체 누구시기에 바람과 물을 호령하시니, 바람과 물조차도 그에게 복종하는가?"
v.WLB: 예수께서 제자들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “너희의 믿음이 어디에 있느냐?” 제자들은 두려움과 놀라움 속에서 서로 말했습니다. “이분이 도대체 누구시기에 바람과 물을 호령하시니 바람과 물조차도 이분께 복종하는가?”

v.CNV: 船到了格拉森人的地区，正在加利利对面，
v.ESV: Then they sailed to the country of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.
v.GAE: 그들이 갈릴리 맞은편 거라사인의 땅에 이르러
v.KJV: And they arrived at the country of the Gadarenes, which is over against Galilee.
v.NASB: Then they sailed to the country of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.
v.NIV: They sailed to the region of the Gerasenes, which is across the lake from Galilee.
v.NRSV: Then they arrived at the country of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 갈릴리 맞은 편에 있는 거라사 지방에 닿았다.
v.WLB: 예수의 일행은 갈릴리 호수 건너편 거라사 지방으로 배를 저어 갔습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣一上岸，就有城里一个被鬼附着的人，迎面而来。这人已经很久不穿衣服，不住在家里，只住在坟墓里。
v.ESV: When Jesus had stepped out on land, there met him a man from the city who had demons. For a long time he had worn no clothes, and he had not lived in a house but among the tombs.
v.GAE: 예수께서 육지에 내리시매 그 도시 사람으로서 귀신 들린 자 하나가 예수를 만나니 그 사람은 오래 옷을 입지 아니하며 집에 거하지도 아니하고 무덤 사이에 거하는 자라
v.KJV: And when he went forth to land, there met him out of the city a certain man, which had devils long time, and ware no clothes, neither abode in any house, but in the tombs.
v.NASB: And when He came out onto the land, He was met by a man from the city who was possessed with demons; and who had not put on any clothing for a long time, and was not living in a house, but in the tombs.
v.NIV: When Jesus stepped ashore, he was met by a demon-possessed man from the town. For a long time this man had not worn clothes or lived in a house, but had lived in the tombs.
v.NRSV: As he stepped out on land, a man of the city who had demons met him. For a long time he had worn no clothes, and he did not live in a house but in the tombs.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 뭍에 내리시니, 그 마을 출신으로서 귀신 들린 사람 하나가 예수를 만났다. 그는 오랫동안 옷을 입지 않은 채, 집에서 살지 않고, 무덤에서 지내고 있었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 배에서 내리시자 그 마을에 사는 귀신 들린 사람과 마주치셨습니다. 그는 옷도 입지 않은 채 집이 아닌 무덤에서 산 지 벌써 오래된 사람이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他一见耶稣，就俯伏喊叫，大声说：“至高　神的儿子耶稣，我跟你有什么关系呢？求你不要使我受苦。”
v.ESV: When he saw Jesus, he cried out and fell down before him and said with a loud voice, “What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, do not torment me.”
v.GAE: 예수를 보고 부르짖으며 그 앞에 엎드려 큰 소리로 불러 이르되 지극히 높으신 하나님의 아들 예수여 당신이 나와 무슨 상관이 있나이까 당신께 구하노니 나를 괴롭게 하지 마옵소서 하니
v.KJV: When he saw Jesus, he cried out, and fell down before him, and with a loud voice said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God most high? I beseech thee, torment me not.
v.NASB: Seeing Jesus, he cried out and fell before Him, and said in a loud voice, 'What business do we have with each other, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg You, do not torment me.'
v.NIV: When he saw Jesus, he cried out and fell at his feet, shouting at the top of his voice, “What do you want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, don’t torture me!”
v.NRSV: When he saw Jesus, he fell down before him and shouted at the top of his voice, “What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, do not torment me” —
v.SAENEW: 그가 예수를 보고, 소리를 지르고서, 그 앞에 엎드려서, 큰 소리로 말하였다. "더없이 높으신 하나님의 아들 예수님, 당신이 나와 무슨 상관이 있습니까? 제발 나를 괴롭히지 마십시오."
v.WLB: 그가 예수를 보자 소리를 지르며 예수의 발 앞에 엎드려 “지극히 높으신 하나님의 아들 예수여, 내가 당신과 무슨 상관이 있습니까? 제발 저를 괴롭히지 마십시오!”라고 찢어질 듯 큰 소리로 외쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 因为耶稣已经吩咐这污灵从那人身上出来。原来这污灵屡次抓住那人；那人被铁链和脚链捆锁，而且有人看管，他竟挣断锁链，被鬼赶入旷野。
v.ESV: For he had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. (For many a time it had seized him. He was kept under guard and bound with chains and shackles, but he would break the bonds and be driven by the demon into the desert.)
v.GAE: 이는 예수께서 이미 더러운 귀신을 명하사 그 사람에게서 나오라 하셨음이라 (귀신이 가끔 그 사람을 붙잡으므로 그를 쇠사슬과 고랑에 매어 지켰으되 그 맨 것을 끊고 귀신에게 몰려 광야로 나갔더라)
v.KJV: (For he had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For oftentimes it had caught him: and he was kept bound with chains and in fetters; and he brake the bands, and was driven of the devil into the wilderness.)
v.NASB: For He had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For it had seized him many times; and he was bound with chains and shackles and kept under guard, and yet he would break his bonds and be driven by the demon into the desert.
v.NIV: For Jesus had commanded the impure spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had seized him, and though he was chained hand and foot and kept under guard, he had broken his chains and had been driven by the demon into solitary places.
v.NRSV: for Jesus had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. (For many times it had seized him; he was kept under guard and bound with chains and shackles, but he would break the bonds and be driven by the demon into the wilds.)
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 이미 악한 귀신더러 그 사람에게서 나가라고 명하셨던 것이다. 귀신이 여러 번 그 사람을 붙잡았기 때문에, 사람들이 그를 쇠사슬과 쇠고랑으로 묶어서 감시하였으나, 그는 그것을 끊고, 귀신에게 몰려서 광야로 뛰쳐나가곤 하였다.
v.WLB: 이는 예수께서 그 사람에게서 나가라고 더러운 영에게 명령하셨기 때문입니다. (그 사람은 수시로 귀신에게 붙들렸는데 손발을 쇠사슬에 묶어 둬도 다 끊어 버리고 귀신에 이끌려 광야로 뛰쳐나가곤 했습니다.)

v.CNV: 耶稣问他：“你叫什么名字？”他说：“群。”因为进到那人里面的鬼很多。
v.ESV: Jesus then asked him, “What is your name?” And he said, “Legion,” for many demons had entered him.
v.GAE: 예수께서 네 이름이 무엇이냐 물으신즉 이르되 군대라 하니 이는 많은 귀신이 들렸음이라
v.KJV: And Jesus asked him, saying, What is thy name? And he said, Legion: because many devils were entered into him.
v.NASB: And Jesus asked him, 'What is your name?' And he said, 'Legion'; for many demons had entered him.
v.NIV: Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” “Legion,” he replied, because many demons had gone into him.
v.NRSV: Jesus then asked him, “What is your name?” He said, “Legion”; for many demons had entered him.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그에게 물으셨다. "네 이름이 무엇이냐?" 그가 대답하였다. "군대입니다." 많은 귀신이 그 사람 속에 들어가 있었기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 그에게 물으셨습니다. “네 이름이 무엇이냐?” 그가 대답했습니다. “‘군대’입니다.” 그 사람 속에 귀신들이 많이 들어 있었기 때문입니다.

v.CNV: 他们求耶稣，不要赶他们进入无底坑。
v.ESV: And they begged him not to command them to depart into the abyss.
v.GAE: 무저갱으로 들어가라 하지 마시기를 간구하더니
v.KJV: And they besought him that he would not command them to go out into the deep.
v.NASB: They were imploring Him not to command them to go away into the abyss.
v.NIV: And they begged Jesus repeatedly not to order them to go into the Abyss.
v.NRSV: They begged him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
v.SAENEW: 귀신들은 자기들을 지옥에 보내지 말아달라고 예수께 간청하였다.
v.WLB: 귀신들은 자기들을 지옥으로 보내지 말아 달라고 예수께 간청했습니다.

v.CNV: 那里有一大群猪正在山上吃东西。他们求耶稣准他们进入猪群。耶稣准许了，
v.ESV: Now a large herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside, and they begged him to let them enter these. So he gave them permission.
v.GAE: 마침 그 곳에 많은 돼지 떼가 산에서 먹고 있는지라 귀신들이 그 돼지에게로 들어가게 허락하심을 간구하니 이에 허락하시니
v.KJV: And there was there an herd of many swine feeding on the mountain: and they besought him that he would suffer them to enter into them. And he suffered them.
v.NASB: Now there was a herd of many swine feeding there on the mountain; and the demons implored Him to permit them to enter the swine. And He gave them permission.
v.NIV: A large herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs, and he gave them permission.
v.NRSV: Now there on the hillside a large herd of swine was feeding; and the demons begged Jesus to let them enter these. So he gave them permission.
v.SAENEW: 마침 그 곳 산기슭에, 놓아 기르는 큰 돼지 떼가 있었다. 귀신들은 자기들을 그 돼지들 속으로 들어가게 허락해 달라고 예수께 간청하였다. 예수께서 허락하시니,
v.WLB: 마침 많은 돼지 떼가 언덕에서 먹이를 먹고 있었습니다. 귀신들이 예수께 돼지들 속으로 들어가게 해 달라고 애원하자 예수께서 허락하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 鬼就从那人身上出来，进入猪群，猪群闯下山崖，掉在海里淹死了。
v.ESV: Then the demons came out of the man and entered the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
v.GAE: 귀신들이 그 사람에게서 나와 돼지에게로 들어가니 그 떼가 비탈로 내리달아 호수에 들어가 몰사하거늘
v.KJV: Then went the devils out of the man, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the lake, and were choked.
v.NASB: And the demons came out of the man and entered the swine; and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and was drowned.
v.NIV: When the demons came out of the man, they went into the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and was drowned.
v.NRSV: Then the demons came out of the man and entered the swine, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and was drowned.
v.SAENEW: 귀신들이 그 사람에게서 나와서, 돼지들 속으로 들어갔다. 그래서 그 돼지 떼는 비탈을 내리달아서 호수에 빠져서 죽었다.
v.WLB: 귀신들은 그 사람에게서 나와 돼지들에게 들어갔습니다. 그러자 돼지 떼는 비탈진 둑으로 내리달아 호수에 빠져 죽게 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 放猪的看见所发生的事就逃跑，到城里和各乡村把这事传开。
v.ESV: When the herdsmen saw what had happened, they fled and told it in the city and in the country.
v.GAE: 치던 자들이 그 이루어진 일을 보고 도망하여 성내와 마을에 알리니
v.KJV: When they that fed them saw what was done, they fled, and went and told it in the city and in the country.
v.NASB: When the herdsmen saw what had happened, they ran away and reported it in the city and out in the country.
v.NIV: When those tending the pigs saw what had happened, they ran off and reported this in the town and countryside,
v.NRSV: When the swineherds saw what had happened, they ran off and told it in the city and in the country.
v.SAENEW: 돼지를 치던 사람들이 이 일을 보고, 도망가서 읍내와 촌에 알렸다.
v.WLB: 돼지를 치던 사람들이 그 광경을 보고 달아나 마을과 그 일대에 이 일을 알렸습니다.

v.CNV: 众人就出来看发生了什么事。来到耶稣那里，看见鬼已经离开的那人，穿着衣服，神志清醒，坐在耶稣脚前，他们就害怕。
v.ESV: Then people went out to see what had happened, and they came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone, sitting at the feet of Jesus, clothed and in his right mind, and they were afraid.
v.GAE: 사람들이 그 이루어진 일을 보러 나와서 예수께 이르러 귀신 나간 사람이 옷을 입고 정신이 온전하여 예수의 발치에 앉아 있는 것을 보고 두려워하거늘
v.KJV: Then they went out to see what was done; and came to Jesus, and found the man, out of whom the devils were departed, sitting at the feet of Jesus, clothed, and in his right mind: and they were afraid.
v.NASB: The people went out to see what had happened; and they came to Jesus, and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting down at the feet of Jesus, clothed and in his right mind; and they became frightened.
v.NIV: and the people went out to see what had happened. When they came to Jesus, they found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting at Jesus’ feet, dressed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.
v.NRSV: Then people came out to see what had happened, and when they came to Jesus, they found the man from whom the demons had gone sitting at the feet of Jesus, clothed and in his right mind. And they were afraid.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 사람들이 일어난 그 일을 보러 나왔다. 그들은 예수께로 와서, 귀신들이 나가버린 그 사람이 옷을 입고 제정신이 들어서 예수의 발 앞에 앉아 있는 것을 보고, 두려워하였다.
v.WLB: 그래서 사람들도 이 광경을 보려고 나왔습니다. 그들이 예수께 가 보니 정말 그 귀신 들렸던 사람이 제정신이 들어 옷을 입고 예수의 발 앞에 앉아 있는 것이었습니다. 그러자 그들은 두려웠습니다.

v.CNV: 当时看见的人，把被鬼附过的人怎样得到医治，说给他们听。
v.ESV: And those who had seen it told them how the demon-possessed man had been healed.
v.GAE: 귀신 들렸던 자가 어떻게 구원 받았는지를 본 자들이 그들에게 이르매
v.KJV: They also which saw it told them by what means he that was possessed of the devils was healed.
v.NASB: Those who had seen it reported to them how the man who was demon-possessed had been made well.
v.NIV: Those who had seen it told the people how the demon-possessed man had been cured.
v.NRSV: Those who had seen it told them how the one who had been possessed by demons had been healed.
v.SAENEW: 처음부터 지켜본 사람들이, 귀신 들렸던 사람이 어떻게 해서 낫게 되었는가를 그들에게 알려 주었다.
v.WLB: 처음부터 이 광경을 지켜본 사람들은 그 귀신 들렸던 사람이 어떻게 낫게 됐는지 그들에게 말했습니다.

v.CNV: 格拉森一带的人，都要求耶稣离开他们，因为他们大大惧怕。他就上船回去了。
v.ESV: Then all the people of the surrounding country of the Gerasenes asked him to depart from them, for they were seized with great fear. So he got into the boat and returned.
v.GAE: 거라사인의 땅 근방 모든 백성이 크게 두려워하여 예수께 떠나가시기를 구하더라 예수께서 배에 올라 돌아가실새
v.KJV: Then the whole multitude of the country of the Gadarenes round about besought him to depart from them; for they were taken with great fear: and he went up into the ship, and returned back again.
v.NASB: And all the people of the country of the Gerasenes and the surrounding district asked Him to leave them, for they were gripped with great fear; and He got into a boat and returned.
v.NIV: Then all the people of the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave them, because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and left.
v.NRSV: Then all the people of the surrounding country of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave them; for they were seized with great fear. So he got into the boat and returned.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 거라사 주위의 고을 주민들은 모두 예수께, 자기들에게서 떠나 달라고 간청하였다. 그들이 큰 두려움에 사로잡혔기 때문이다. 그래서 예수께서는 배에 올라 되돌아가시는데,
v.WLB: 그러자 거라사 주변의 모든 사람들은 두려움에 가득 차 예수께 떠나 달라고 간청했습니다. 그리하여 예수께서는 배를 타고 그곳을 떠나셨습니다.

v.CNV: 鬼已经离开的那人求耶稣，要跟他在一起；但耶稣打发他回去，说：
v.ESV: The man from whom the demons had gone begged that he might be with him, but Jesus sent him away, saying,
v.GAE: 귀신 나간 사람이 함께 있기를 구하였으나 예수께서 그를 보내시며 이르시되
v.KJV: Now the man out of whom the devils were departed besought him that he might be with him: but Jesus sent him away, saying,
v.NASB: But the man from whom the demons had gone out was begging Him that he might accompany Him; but He sent him away, saying,
v.NIV: The man from whom the demons had gone out begged to go with him, but Jesus sent him away, saying,
v.NRSV: The man from whom the demons had gone begged that he might be with him; but Jesus sent him away, saying,
v.SAENEW: 귀신이 나간 그 사람이 예수와 함께 있게 해 달라고 애원하였으나, 예수께서는 그를 돌려보내시며 이렇게 말씀하셨다.
v.WLB: 귀신 들렸던 그 사람은 자신도 함께 가겠다고 예수께 애원했습니다. 그러나 예수께서는 그를 보내며 말씀하셨습니다.

v.CNV: “你回家去，述说　神为你作了怎样的事。”他就走遍全城，传讲耶稣为他作了怎样的事。
v.ESV: “Return to your home, and declare how much God has done for you.” And he went away, proclaiming throughout the whole city how much Jesus had done for him.
v.GAE: 집으로 돌아가 하나님이 네게 어떻게 큰 일을 행하셨는지를 말하라 하시니 그가 가서 예수께서 자기에게 어떻게 큰 일을 행하셨는지를 온 성내에 전파하니라
v.KJV: Return to thine own house, and shew how great things God hath done unto thee. And he went his way, and published throughout the whole city how great things Jesus had done unto him.
v.NASB: 'Return to your house and describe what great things God has done for you.' So he went away, proclaiming throughout the whole city what great things Jesus had done for him.
v.NIV: “Return home and tell how much God has done for you.” So the man went away and told all over town how much Jesus had done for him.
v.NRSV: “Return to your home, and declare how much God has done for you.” So he went away, proclaiming throughout the city how much Jesus had done for him.
v.SAENEW: "네 집으로 돌아가서, 하나님께서 네게 하신 일을 다 이야기하여라." 그 사람이 떠나가서, 예수께서 자기에게 하신 일을 낱낱이 온 읍내에 알렸다.
v.WLB: “집으로 돌아가서 하나님께서 네게 하신 일을 다 말하여라.” 그러자 그 사람은 온 마을을 다니며 예수께서 얼마나 큰일을 행하셨는지 전했습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣回来的时候，众人欢迎他，因为大家都在等着他。
v.ESV: Now when Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.
v.GAE: 예수께서 돌아오시매 무리가 환영하니 이는 다 기다렸음이러라
v.KJV: And it came to pass, that, when Jesus was returned, the people gladly received him: for they were all waiting for him.
v.NASB: And as Jesus returned, the people welcomed Him, for they had all been waiting for Him.
v.NIV: Now when Jesus returned, a crowd welcomed him, for they were all expecting him.
v.NRSV: Now when Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 돌아오시니, 무리가 그를 환영하였다. 그들은 모두 예수를 기다리고 있었던 것이다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 돌아오시자 많은 사람들이 그분을 반겼습니다. 그들 모두가 예수를 기다리고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 那时，有一个人来了，名叫叶鲁，他是一位会堂主管。他俯伏在耶稣脚前，求他往他家里去，
v.ESV: And there came a man named Jairus, who was a ruler of the synagogue. And falling at Jesus' feet, he implored him to come to his house,
v.GAE: 이에 회당장인 야이로라 하는 사람이 와서 예수의 발 아래에 엎드려 자기 집에 오시기를 간구하니
v.KJV: And, behold, there came a man named Jairus, and he was a ruler of the synagogue: and he fell down at Jesus’ feet, and besought him that he would come into his house:
v.NASB: And there came a man named Jairus, and he was an official of the synagogue; and he fell at Jesus' feet, and began to implore Him to come to his house;
v.NIV: Then a man named Jairus, a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus’ feet, pleading with him to come to his house
v.NRSV: Just then there came a man named Jairus, a leader of the synagogue. He fell at Jesus’ feet and begged him to come to his house,
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 야이로라는 사람이 왔다. 이 사람은 회당장이었다. 그가 예수의 발 앞에 엎드려서, 자기 집으로 가시자고 간청하였다.
v.WLB: 그때 야이로라는 회당장이 와서 예수의 발 앞에 엎드리며 자기 집에 와 달라고 간절히 애원했습니다.

v.CNV: 因为他的独生女，约十二岁，快要死了。耶稣去的时候，群众拥挤着他。
v.ESV: for he had an only daughter, about twelve years of age, and she was dying. As Jesus went, the people pressed around him.
v.GAE: 이는 자기에게 열두 살 된 외딸이 있어 죽어감이러라예수께서 가실 때에 무리가 밀려들더라
v.KJV: For he had one only daughter, about twelve years of age, and she lay a dying. But as he went the people thronged him.
v.NASB: for he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, and she was dying. But as He went, the crowds were pressing against Him.
v.NIV: because his only daughter, a girl of about twelve, was dying. As Jesus was on his way, the crowds almost crushed him.
v.NRSV: for he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, who was dying. As he went, the crowds pressed in on him.
v.SAENEW: 그에게 열두 살쯤 된 외동딸이 있는데, 그 딸이 죽어가고 있었기 때문이다. 예수께서 야이로의 집으로 가시는데, 무리가 예수를 밀어댔다.
v.WLB: 열두 살 된 자기 외동딸이 죽어 가고 있었기 때문입니다. 예수께서 그리로 내려가시는데 많은 사람들이 밀어 댔습니다.

v.CNV: 有一个女人，患了十二年的血漏病，在医生手里花尽了全部养生的（有些抄本无“在医生手里花尽了全部养生的”一句），没有一个能医好她。
v.ESV: And there was a woman who had had a discharge of blood for twelve years, and though she had spent all her living on physicians, she could not be healed by anyone.
v.GAE: 이에 열두 해를 혈루증으로 앓는 중에 아무에게도 고침을 받지 못하던 여자가
v.KJV: And a woman having an issue of blood twelve years, which had spent all her living upon physicians, neither could be healed of any,
v.NASB: And a woman who had a hemorrhage for twelve years, and could not be healed by anyone,
v.NIV: And a woman was there who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years, but no one could heal her.
v.NRSV: Now there was a woman who had been suffering from hemorrhages for twelve years; and though she had spent all she had on physicians, no one could cure her.
v.SAENEW: 무리 가운데 열두 해 동안 혈루증으로 앓는 여자가 있었는데 [의사에게 재산을 모두 다 탕진했지만] 아무도 이 여자를 고쳐주지 못하였다.
v.WLB: 그중에는 12년 동안 혈루병을 앓아 온 여인이 있었는데 의사들에게 재산을 모두 썼지만 어느 누구도 그 여인의 병을 고쳐 줄 수 없었습니다.

v.CNV: 她从后面挤来，一摸耶稣的衣裳繸子，血就立刻止住。
v.ESV: She came up behind him and touched the fringe of his garment, and immediately her discharge of blood ceased.
v.GAE: 예수의 뒤로 와서 그의 옷 가에 손을 대니 혈루증이 즉시 그쳤더라
v.KJV: Came behind him, and touched the border of his garment: and immediately her issue of blood stanched.
v.NASB: came up behind Him and touched the fringe of His cloak, and immediately her hemorrhage stopped.
v.NIV: She came up behind him and touched the edge of his cloak, and immediately her bleeding stopped.
v.NRSV: She came up behind him and touched the fringe of his clothes, and immediately her hemorrhage stopped.
v.SAENEW: 이 여자가 뒤에서 다가와서는 예수의 옷술에 손을 대니, 곧 출혈이 그쳤다.
v.WLB: 그 여인은 예수의 뒤로 비집고 다가가 그분의 옷자락에 손을 댔습니다. 그러자 즉시 출혈이 멈췄습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“摸我的是谁？”众人都不承认。彼得说：“主啊，众人都拥挤着你。”
v.ESV: And Jesus said, “Who was it that touched me?” When all denied it, Peter said, “Master, the crowds surround you and are pressing in on you!”
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 내게 손을 댄 자가 누구냐 하시니 다 아니라 할 때에 베드로가 이르되 주여 무리가 밀려들어 미나이다
v.KJV: And Jesus said, Who touched me? When all denied, Peter and they that were with him said, Master, the multitude throng thee and press thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?
v.NASB: And Jesus said, 'Who is the one who touched Me?' And while they were all denying it, Peter said, 'Master, the people are crowding and pressing in on You.'
v.NIV: “Who touched me?” Jesus asked. When they all denied it, Peter said, “Master, the people are crowding and pressing against you.”
v.NRSV: Then Jesus asked, “Who touched me?” When all denied it, Peter said, “Master, the crowds surround you and press in on you.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 물으셨다. "내게 손을 댄 사람이 누구냐?" 사람들이 모두 부인하는데, 베드로가 말하였다. "선생님, 무리가 선생님을 에워싸서 밀치고 있습니다."
v.WLB: 그때 예수께서 “누가 내게 손을 댔느냐?”라고 물으셨습니다. 사람들이 모두 만지지 않았다고 하자 베드로가 말했습니다. “선생님, 많은 사람들이 선생님을 밀어 대고 있습니다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“必定有人摸我，因为我觉得有能力从我身上出去。”
v.ESV: But Jesus said, “Someone touched me, for I perceive that power has gone out from me.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 내게 손을 댄 자가 있도다 이는 내게서 능력이 나간 줄 앎이로다 하신대
v.KJV: And Jesus said, Somebody hath touched me: for I perceive that virtue is gone out of me.
v.NASB: But Jesus said, 'Someone did touch Me, for I was aware that power had gone out of Me.'
v.NIV: But Jesus said, “Someone touched me; I know that power has gone out from me.”
v.NRSV: But Jesus said, “Someone touched me; for I noticed that power had gone out from me.”
v.SAENEW: 그러자 예수께서 말씀하셨다. "누군가가 내게 손을 댔다. 나는 내게서 능력이 빠져나간 것을 알고 있다."
v.WLB: 그러자 예수께서 말씀하셨습니다. “누군가가 내게 손을 댔다. 내게서 능력이 나간 것을 알고 있다.”

v.CNV: 那女人见不能隐瞒，就战战兢兢地过来，向他俯伏，把摸他的缘故，和怎样立刻得到医治，在众人面前说出来。
v.ESV: And when the woman saw that she was not hidden, she came trembling, and falling down before him declared in the presence of all the people why she had touched him, and how she had been immediately healed.
v.GAE: 여자가 스스로 숨기지 못할 줄 알고 떨며 나아와 엎드리어 그 손 댄 이유와 곧 나은 것을 모든 사람 앞에서 말하니
v.KJV: And when the woman saw that she was not hid, she came trembling, and falling down before him, she declared unto him before all the people for what cause she had touched him and how she was healed immediately.
v.NASB: When the woman saw that she had not escaped notice, she came trembling and fell down before Him, and declared in the presence of all the people the reason why she had touched Him, and how she had been immediately healed.
v.NIV: Then the woman, seeing that she could not go unnoticed, came trembling and fell at his feet. In the presence of all the people, she told why she had touched him and how she had been instantly healed.
v.NRSV: When the woman saw that she could not remain hidden, she came trembling; and falling down before him, she declared in the presence of all the people why she had touched him, and how she had been immediately healed.
v.SAENEW: 그 여자는 더 이상 숨길 수 없음을 알고서, 떨면서 나아와 예수께 엎드려서, 그에게 손을 댄 이유와 또 곧 낫게 된 경위를 모든 백성 앞에 알렸다.
v.WLB: 그러자 여인은 더 이상 숨길 수 없음을 알고 떨면서 앞으로 나와 예수의 발 앞에 엎드렸습니다. 여인은 모든 사람 앞에서 왜 예수께 손을 댔는지, 그리고 어떻게 병이 즉시 나았는지 말했습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣对她说：“女儿，你的信使你痊愈了，平安地去吧！”
v.ESV: And he said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 딸아 네 믿음이 너를 구원하였으니 평안히 가라 하시더라
v.KJV: And he said unto her, Daughter, be of good comfort: thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace.
v.NASB: And He said to her, 'Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace.'
v.NIV: Then he said to her, “Daughter, your faith has healed you. Go in peace.”
v.NRSV: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace.”
v.SAENEW: 그러자 예수께서 그 여자에게 말씀하셨다. "딸아, 네 믿음이 너를 구원하였다. 평안히 가거라."
v.WLB: 그러자 예수께서 여인에게 말씀하셨습니다. “딸아, 네 믿음이 너를 구원했다. 평안히 가거라.”

v.CNV: 耶稣还在说话的时候，有人从会堂主管家里来，说：“你的女儿死了，不必再劳动老师了。”
v.ESV: While he was still speaking, someone from the ruler's house came and said, “Your daughter is dead; do not trouble the Teacher any more.”
v.GAE: 아직 말씀하실 때에 회당장의 집에서 사람이 와서 말하되 당신의 딸이 죽었나이다 선생님을 더 괴롭게 하지 마소서 하거늘
v.KJV: While he yet spake, there cometh one from the ruler of the synagogue’s house, saying to him, Thy daughter is dead; trouble not the Master.
v.NASB: While He was still speaking, someone came from the house of the synagogue official, saying, 'Your daughter has died; do not trouble the Teacher anymore.'
v.NIV: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the house of Jairus, the synagogue leader. “Your daughter is dead,” he said. “Don’t bother the teacher anymore.”
v.NRSV: While he was still speaking, someone came from the leader’s house to say, “Your daughter is dead; do not trouble the teacher any longer.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 아직 말씀을 계속하시는데, 회당장의 집에서 사람이 와서 말하였다. "따님이 죽었습니다. 선생님을 더 괴롭히지 마십시오."
v.WLB: 예수께서 말씀을 채 마치시기도 전에 야이로 회당장의 집에서 사람이 와서 말했습니다. “따님이 죽었습니다. 선생님께 더 폐를 끼치지 않는 것이 좋겠습니다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣听见就对他说：“不要怕，只要信，她必得痊愈。”
v.ESV: But Jesus on hearing this answered him, “Do not fear; only believe, and she will be well.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 들으시고 이르시되 두려워하지 말고 믿기만 하라 그리하면 딸이 구원을 얻으리라 하시고
v.KJV: But when Jesus heard it, he answered him, saying, Fear not: believe only, and she shall be made whole.
v.NASB: But when Jesus heard this, He answered him, 'Do not be afraid any longer; only believe, and she will be made well.'
v.NIV: Hearing this, Jesus said to Jairus, “Don’t be afraid; just believe, and she will be healed.”
v.NRSV: When Jesus heard this, he replied, “Do not fear. Only believe, and she will be saved.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 들으시고 나서, 회당장에게 말씀하셨다. "두려워하지 말고, 믿기만 하여라. 딸이 나을 것이다."
v.WLB: 이 말을 듣고 예수께서 그에게 말씀하셨습니다. “두려워하지 마라. 믿기만 하면 아이가 나을 것이다.”

v.CNV: 到了那家，除了彼得、约翰、雅各和女孩的父母以外，他不准任何人同他进屋里去。
v.ESV: And when he came to the house, he allowed no one to enter with him, except Peter and John and James, and the father and mother of the child.
v.GAE: 그 집에 이르러 베드로와 요한과 야고보와 아이의 부모 외에는 함께 들어가기를 허락하지 아니하시니라
v.KJV: And when he came into the house, he suffered no man to go in, save Peter, and James, and John, and the father and the mother of the maiden.
v.NASB: When He came to the house, He did not allow anyone to enter with Him, except Peter and John and James, and the girl's father and mother.
v.NIV: When he arrived at the house of Jairus, he did not let anyone go in with him except Peter, John and James, and the child’s father and mother.
v.NRSV: When he came to the house, he did not allow anyone to enter with him, except Peter, John, and James, and the child’s father and mother.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 그 집에 이르러서, 베드로와 요한과 야고보와 그 아이의 부모 밖에는, 아무도 함께 들어가는 것을 허락하지 않으셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 야이로의 집에 이르러 베드로와 요한과 야고보와 아이의 부모 외에는 아무도 들어오지 못하게 하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 众人都在痛哭哀号，他说：“不要哭！她不是死了，而是睡着了。”
v.ESV: And all were weeping and mourning for her, but he said, “Do not weep, for she is not dead but sleeping.”
v.GAE: 모든 사람이 아이를 위하여 울며 통곡하매 예수께서 이르시되 울지 말라 죽은 것이 아니라 잔다 하시니
v.KJV: And all wept, and bewailed her: but he said, Weep not; she is not dead, but sleepeth.
v.NASB: Now they were all weeping and lamenting for her; but He said, 'Stop weeping, for she has not died, but is asleep.'
v.NIV: Meanwhile, all the people were wailing and mourning for her. “Stop wailing,” Jesus said. “She is not dead but asleep.”
v.NRSV: They were all weeping and wailing for her; but he said, “Do not weep; for she is not dead but sleeping.”
v.SAENEW: 사람들은 모두 울며 그 아이에 대해 슬퍼하고 있었다. 예수께서 말씀하셨다. "울지 말아라. 아이가 죽은 것이 아니라, 자고 있다."
v.WLB: 사람들은 모두 그 아이에 대해 애도하며 크게 울고 있었습니다. 예수께서 말씀하셨습니다. “울지 마라. 이 아이는 죽은 것이 아니라 자고 있다.”

v.CNV: 他们明知女孩已经死了，就嘲笑他。
v.ESV: And they laughed at him, knowing that she was dead.
v.GAE: 그들이 그 죽은 것을 아는 고로 비웃더라
v.KJV: And they laughed him to scorn, knowing that she was dead.
v.NASB: And they began laughing at Him, knowing that she had died.
v.NIV: They laughed at him, knowing that she was dead.
v.NRSV: And they laughed at him, knowing that she was dead.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 아이가 죽었음을 알고 있으므로, 예수를 비웃었다.
v.WLB: 그들은 아이가 죽은 것을 알기에 예수를 비웃었습니다.

v.CNV: 他进去拉着女孩的手，叫她说：“孩子，起来！”
v.ESV: But taking her by the hand he called, saying, “Child, arise.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 아이의 손을 잡고 불러 이르시되 아이야 일어나라 하시니
v.KJV: And he put them all out, and took her by the hand, and called, saying, Maid, arise.
v.NASB: He, however, took her by the hand and called, saying, 'Child, arise!'
v.NIV: But he took her by the hand and said, “My child, get up!”
v.NRSV: But he took her by the hand and called out, “Child, get up!”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 아이의 손을 잡으시고 말씀하셨다. "아이야, 일어나라."
v.WLB: 그러나 예수께서 그 아이의 손을 잡고 말씀하셨습니다. “아이야, 일어나라!”

v.CNV: 她的灵魂回来了，她就立刻起来。耶稣吩咐给她东西吃。
v.ESV: And her spirit returned, and she got up at once. And he directed that something should be given her to eat.
v.GAE: 그 영이 돌아와 아이가 곧 일어나거늘 예수께서 먹을 것을 주라 명하시니
v.KJV: And her spirit came again, and she arose straightway: and he commanded to give her meat.
v.NASB: And her spirit returned, and she got up immediately; and He gave orders for something to be given her to eat.
v.NIV: Her spirit returned, and at once she stood up. Then Jesus told them to give her something to eat.
v.NRSV: Her spirit returned, and she got up at once. Then he directed them to give her something to eat.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 그 아이의 영이 돌아와서, 아이가 곧 일어났다. 예수께서는 먹을 것을 아이에게 주라고 지시하셨다.
v.WLB: 그때 그 아이의 영이 돌아와 아이가 곧 일어났습니다. 그러자 예수께서 아이에게 먹을 것을 갖다 주라고 말씀하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 她父母非常惊奇。耶稣嘱咐他们不要把他所作的事告诉人。
v.ESV: And her parents were amazed, but he charged them to tell no one what had happened.
v.GAE: 그 부모가 놀라는지라 예수께서 경고하사 이 일을 아무에게도 말하지 말라 하시니라
v.KJV: And her parents were astonished: but he charged them that they should tell no man what was done.
v.NASB: Her parents were amazed; but He instructed them to tell no one what had happened.
v.NIV: Her parents were astonished, but he ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened.
v.NRSV: Her parents were astounded; but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
v.SAENEW: 아이의 부모는 놀랐다. 예수께서 이 일을 아무에게도 말하지 말라고 그들에게 명하셨다.
v.WLB: 그 아이의 부모는 무척 놀랐습니다. 그러나 예수께서는 이 일을 아무에게도 말하지 말라고 그들에게 명령하셨습니다.
